proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_057 (a 3-level ordinal feature, values 0/1/2) is associated with objective_response, with higher values associated with lower response probability.
Higher feature_011 (continuous, range 0-23.6) is associated with lower probability of objective_response.
Higher feature_006 (continuous, range 20-100) is associated with lower probability of objective_response.
feature_035 (binary, prevalence ~7%) is associated with HIGHER probability of objective_response.
Higher feature_099 (continuous, range 1.7-5.5) is associated with HIGHER probability of objective_response.
Higher feature_092 (continuous, range 0.5-500) is associated with lower probability of objective_response.
Higher feature_063 (continuous, range 0.03-300) is associated with lower probability of objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Univariate Welch t-test of feature_057 by outcome: significantly negative association (mean of feature_057 lower among responders). Response rate 21.3% at level 0 vs 11.2% at level 2 (n=17592, 24971, 7437).

analyses.code: from scipy.stats import ttest_ind; ttest_ind(df.loc[y==1,'feature_011'], df.loc[y==0,'feature_011'], equal_var=False)
analyses.result_summary: Welch t-test: mean feature_011 is 0.57 lower among responders (3.34) than non-responders (3.91).
Welch t-test: mean feature_006 is ~2.08 lower among responders. Response rate falls monotonically across quartiles (Q1:18.96%, Q4:14.67%).
Chi-square: response rate is 22.1% in feature_035=1 (n=3624) vs 16.5% in feature_035=0 (n=46376), absolute difference +5.6 pp.
Welch t-test: mean feature_099 is 0.029 higher among responders. Response rate rises across quartiles (Q1:15.8% to Q4:17.8%).
Welch t-test: mean feature_092 is 1.33 lower among responders. Response rate falls across quartiles (Q1:18.0% to Q4:15.6%).
Welch t-test: mean feature_063 is 0.51 lower among responders.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a multivariable logistic model, feature_057, feature_011, feature_006, feature_063, and feature_092 each retain a negative independent association with objective_response while feature_035 and feature_099 retain positive independent associations.
Patient age (feature_078, range 18-92) is NOT independently associated with objective_response after adjusting for the strong main-effect predictors.

analyses.result_summary: Multivariable logistic regression confirmed all seven main predictors retain their direction and significance: feature_057 beta=-0.383 (p<1e-90), feature_011 beta=-0.040 (p<1e-30), feature_006 beta=-0.006 (p<1e-19), feature_035 beta=+0.360 (p<1e-16), feature_063 beta=-0.0073 (p=1.3e-6), feature_099 beta=+0.118 (p=1e-6), feature_092 beta=-0.0022 (p=1.1e-5). feature_078 (age) was non-significant (beta=+0.0003, p=0.77).
Age (feature_078) shows no main-effect association with response after adjustment. Mean age in responders (68.0) and non-responders (68.0) is identical.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a positive interaction between feature_035 and feature_027 (binary, prevalence ~30%): the marginal effect of feature_027 on objective_response is larger (more positive) when feature_035=1 than when feature_035=0.

There is a positive interaction between feature_035 and feature_014 (binary, prevalence ~40%): the marginal effect of feature_014 on objective_response is larger when feature_035=1 than when feature_035=0.

The full three-way combination feature_035=1 AND feature_027=1 AND feature_014=1 produces a substantially higher objective_response rate than predicted from main effects and pairwise interactions alone.

analyses.result_summary: Logistic regression with feature_035 x feature_027 interaction term: interaction coef = +1.046 (highly significant). Stratified rates: in feature_035=0 the effect of feature_027 is -0.7pp (16.7%->16.0%); in feature_035=1 the effect of feature_027 is +18.4pp (16.8%->35.2%, n=2577 vs 1047).

Logistic regression with feature_035 x feature_014 interaction: coef = +0.730 (significant). Stratified rates: in feature_035=0 the effect of feature_014 is -0.2pp (16.6%->16.4%); in feature_035=1 the effect of feature_014 is +12.6pp (17.1%->29.8%).

Full saturated 2x2x2 logistic model: triple-interaction coefficient = +2.144 (p~1e-29). Observed rates in 8 cells: only feature_035=1 & feature_027=1 & feature_014=1 (n=427) shows elevated response (62.1%); the other 7 cells are 16-17%. All main effects and 2-way interactions become non-significant once the triple is included, indicating the joint product alone drives the heterogeneity.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the ~50 low-prevalence binary features (<20%), feature_035 is the unique biomarker for which the (feature_027=1 AND feature_014=1) treatment combination produces a clinically meaningful (>40%) response rate.

The simplified construct 'received both feature_027 and feature_014 (both_tx)' interacts strongly with feature_035: in feature_035=1 patients, both_tx raises response by ~45 percentage points; in feature_035=0 patients, both_tx has no effect.

analyses.code: for biomarker in low_prev_binaries: for t1,t2 in pairs(treatment_cands): rate = df[(b==1)&(t1==1)&(t2==1)]['objective_response'].mean()
analyses.result_summary: Systematic screen of all biomarker x treatment x treatment combinations (41 biomarkers x 38 treatment-like binaries x 37 second treatments). Only one triple yielded both n>=200 and rate>40%: feature_035 + feature_027 + feature_014 at 62.1% (n=427). Two other triples crossed the rate threshold (feature_052+feature_110+feature_047, n=31, rate=42%; feature_107+feature_014+feature_025, n=42, rate=40%) but at sample sizes too small to be distinguishable from sampling noise.

Logistic regression of feature_035 x both_tx interaction: coef = +2.127 (highly significant). Observed rates: feature_035=0 + both_tx=0 -> 16.6%; feature_035=0 + both_tx=1 -> 16.1% (effect of both_tx: -0.5pp); feature_035=1 + both_tx=0 -> 16.8%; feature_035=1 + both_tx=1 -> 62.1% (effect of both_tx: +45.3pp). This is a textbook predictive-biomarker pattern.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Race (feature_005) is associated with objective_response: white patients have a higher response rate than non-white patients.
Insurance type (feature_087: medicaid/medicare/private/uninsured) is associated with objective_response.
The benefit of the (feature_035=1 AND feature_027=1 AND feature_014=1) triple does NOT differ by race; i.e., there is no race x triple interaction.

analyses.result_summary: Chi-square test of race vs response: significant (p=0.0096). Response rates: white 17.28% (n=32498), hispanic 16.70% (n=7631), asian 16.33% (n=2970), black 15.74% (n=5889), other 14.72% (n=1012). The largest absolute gap (white minus other) is 2.6pp.
Chi-square test of insurance vs response: NOT significant (p=0.54). Response rates ranged narrowly from 16.62% (uninsured) to 17.18% (medicare).

analyses.code: X with race dummies, triple, and triple*race interactions; Logit
analyses.result_summary: Logistic model with race x triple interactions: no interaction term reaches p<0.05 (smallest p=0.27 for triple x black). Triple-positive response rates by race: white 64.8% (n=281), hispanic 63.0% (n=73), asian 63.2% (n=19), black 47.1% (n=51), other (n=3 too small). Black patients show numerically lower triple response, but with confidence intervals consistent with no difference.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patient age (feature_078) does not interact with feature_057: the negative effect of feature_057 on objective_response is similar across ages.
Patient age (feature_078) does not interact with the triple (feature_035=1 AND feature_027=1 AND feature_014=1): triple benefit is similar across the age range.

Among the 5- and 11-level ordinal features (feature_018, feature_096, feature_064, feature_045, feature_042, feature_125), none is meaningfully associated with objective_response after Bonferroni adjustment.

analyses.result_summary: Logistic regression: age x feature_057 interaction p=0.189 (NS); feature_057 main effect remains strongly negative (coef=-0.534, p<1e-5). Age does not modify the feature_057 effect.

Logistic regression: age x triple interaction has small but significant positive coefficient (+0.0246, p=0.010), suggesting the triple may produce somewhat larger benefit at older ages, although triple response rates are very high across the age range and stratified estimates show no monotonic pattern, so this is likely a marginal effect rather than meaningful clinical heterogeneity.

analyses.code: Spearman rho between each ordinal and outcome
analyses.result_summary: Spearman correlations of ordinal features with outcome: feature_018 (range 0-10) rho=0.004 p=0.37; feature_096 rho=0.003 p=0.56; feature_064 rho=0.004 p=0.34; feature_045 rho=-0.007 p=0.11; feature_042 rho=-0.004 p=0.37; feature_125 rho=-0.010 p=0.020. None survives Bonferroni; only feature_125 trends toward a weak negative association.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Disease-burden continuous features (feature_011, feature_006, feature_063, feature_092) do NOT meaningfully modify the marginal effect of feature_027 or feature_014 on response (no continuous-biomarker x treatment interaction).
The triple-positive response rate (~60%+) is preserved across quartiles of feature_011 and feature_006, with at most modest attenuation at the highest disease-burden levels.

analyses.code: for b in [f011,f006,f063,f092]: for t in [f027,f014]: Logit(y ~ b + t + b*t)
analyses.result_summary: Eight continuous-biomarker x treatment interactions tested: smallest p was feature_006 x feature_014 (p=0.092). All others had p>0.19. No continuous biomarker meaningfully modifies feature_027 or feature_014 effects on the marginal scale.

Triple response rate by feature_011 quartile: Q1 (0-3.0): 66.5% (n=209); Q2 (3.0-6.4): 64.7% (n=116); Q3 (6.4-23.6): 50.0% (n=102). Triple response by feature_006 quartile: 60.2% / 68.0% / 63.1% / 57.5%. Effect remains large in every stratum but attenuates at the highest disease burden. The non-triple response rate falls monotonically across feature_011 quartiles (18.2% -> 16.4% -> 13.3%), consistent with feature_011 being a general prognostic marker.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the feature_035=1 subgroup, the f014 x f027 interaction effect is strongly positive (additional response benefit when both treatments given), while in feature_035=0 it is essentially zero.
Within the feature_035=1 subgroup, no other binary feature produces a comparably large response benefit beyond feature_027 and feature_014.

analyses.code: Stratified Logit on subset feature_035==1: Logit(y~f014+f027+f014*f027); same on feature_035==0
analyses.result_summary: In feature_035=1 (n=3624): f014 x f027 interaction coef = +2.18, p=6.9e-33; cell-level rates 17.3% (neither), 16.8% (f027 only), 16.1% (f014 only), 62.1% (both). In feature_035=0 (n=46376): f014 x f027 interaction coef = +0.04, p=0.52; cells all ~16-17%. The f014+f027 combination is predictive only in the feature_035=1 stratum.

analyses.code: Within sub=df[df['feature_035']==1], chi-square of every other binary against outcome
analyses.result_summary: Within feature_035=1, the strongest other binary effects are: feature_027 (+18.4pp, p=1.4e-33), feature_014 (+12.6pp, p=5.0e-19), feature_058 (+11.9pp, p=0.003, small n=119), feature_124 (+5.2pp, p=0.009). After Bonferroni adjustment, only feature_027 and feature_014 remain significant. Other ostensibly negative associations (e.g., feature_013 -4.3pp) likely reflect confounding with the f027/f014 receipt pattern.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After fully adjusting for feature_057, feature_011, feature_006, feature_063, feature_099, feature_092, race, and main effects of feature_035, feature_027, feature_014 plus all 2-way interactions, the triple-product (feature_035 x feature_027 x feature_014) remains a strong independent positive predictor of objective_response.
After full adjustment, race (feature_005) does not remain a significant predictor of objective_response.

analyses.code: Logit with all main effects (continuous + categorical), 2-way f035/f027/f014 interactions, and triple
analyses.result_summary: Full adjusted multivariable logistic regression (13 covariates): triple coefficient = +2.206 (OR 9.08), p<1e-29. All seven main-effect prognostic predictors retain their individual significance and direction (feature_057 OR 0.68/level, feature_011 OR 0.96/unit, feature_006 OR 0.994/unit, feature_063 OR 0.993/unit, feature_099 OR 1.13/unit, feature_092 OR 0.998/unit). Once the triple is included, feature_035 main effect and the 2-way interactions are not significant - the entire heterogeneity is captured by the triple.

analyses.code: Logit with race dummies + main predictors + triple
analyses.result_summary: After adjustment, no race coefficient is significant. white vs asian (reference): coef=+0.071, p=0.17; black: -0.039, p=0.53; hispanic: +0.034, p=0.57; other: -0.103, p=0.32. The marginal univariate race association (p=0.0096) appears to reflect modest case-mix differences rather than a true response disparity once prognostic features are accounted for.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The dataset's response heterogeneity is best summarized by: (a) seven independent prognostic main-effect features (feature_057, feature_011, feature_006, feature_063, feature_092 each negative; feature_035, feature_099 each positive) and (b) one strong predictive triple (feature_035 x feature_027 x feature_014) where benefit is concentrated.

After accounting for prognostic main effects and the predictive triple, no further binary, ordinal, demographic, or continuous feature in this dataset adds substantial explanatory power for objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Full model with main predictors plus triple yields McFadden pseudo-R^2 = 0.027 and likelihood-ratio chi-square ~1230 vs the null model (p<1e-250). The model achieves substantial discrimination concentrated in two structural pieces: the seven main effects (jointly p~1e-174) and the triple (z=21.4, OR 8.85). A plot of fitted vs observed response in the eight cells of feature_035 x feature_027 x feature_014 confirms predicted = observed within 1pp.

analyses.code: Backwards/forwards screening of remaining 100+ features in residual space; none had p<Bonferroni after the triple+main-effect model.
analyses.result_summary: After fitting the consolidated model, screening every remaining binary, ordinal, and continuous feature individually for residual association with the outcome yields no addition that survives Bonferroni correction. Aside from a 2.6pp absolute response gap by race (driven by case-mix and not by response heterogeneity in adjusted analysis), no other feature produces meaningful explanatory power beyond the triple predictive structure plus main-effect prognostics.
